title: key_owner empty-string bypasses actor key ownership check
description: In actor_cache.rs get_public_key() line 61, the publicKey.owner check is: if !key_owner.is_empty() && key_owner != actor_uri { bail!(...) }. If the actor document omits publicKey.owner entirely, key_owner is the empty string and the ownership check is silently skipped. A MITM or confused-deputy attack could supply a crafted actor document with no owner field and an arbitrary public key, and the cache would accept it. Fix: require owner to be present and equal to actor_uri, rather than skipping the check when it is absent.
close_reason: Fixed: shared_inbox SSRF guard, key_owner required, webhook media cap. Remaining (replay, unsigned accept, DNS rebinding, body buffer) accepted as known architectural limitations.
